headline: ESSCOM Border Security Faces RM800 Million Funding Gap
context: ESSCOM reported RM800 million shortfall in 2026-2030 operational plan threatening coastal surveillance capabilities.

cards.big: ESSCOM faces RM800 million funding gap for 2026-2030 operations
cards.sub: Coastal surveillance of Sabah eastern seaboard at risk without funding

cards.big: ESSCOM monitors 1,400km coastline with only 12 operational patrol boats
cards.sub: Recommended minimum is 28 boats for adequate coverage of security zone

cards.big: 3 kidnapping incidents in Sulu-Celebes Sea in 2025 involved Malaysian fishermen
cards.sub: Response times averaging 4 hours allowed perpetrators to cross into Philippines

cards.big: Radar coverage of ESSZONE reaches only 65% of designated security area
cards.sub: 8 planned radar stations remain unfunded leaving maritime awareness gaps

cards.big: Funding gaps test whether eastern border security matches the threat assessment
RM800 million ESSCOM funding gap. Border security works only when resourced at the scale the threat demands.
